seoTitle: How to Connect a SharePoint List to Power BI Directly
metaDescription: Learn how to set up a direct connection from a SharePoint list to Power BI so your list data flows into reports without manual exports.
intro: If your team tracks data in a SharePoint list, you don't need to export it manually to build a Power BI report. This tutorial covers the direct connection between SharePoint and Power BI, including the settings needed on both sides.

steps.heading: Review your SharePoint list
steps.body: Confirm the SharePoint list you want to connect has the columns and data structure your report needs.

steps.heading: Connect from Power BI
steps.body: In Power BI, use the SharePoint List connector and enter the site URL where your list lives.

steps.heading: Configure the connection settings
steps.body: Select the specific list and fields you want to import, then set the authentication method for your organization.

steps.heading: Visualize the data
steps.body: Build report visuals using the imported list data, such as tracking project status or customer feedback.

faq.question: Does this connection require any special SharePoint permissions?
faq.answer: You need at least read access to the SharePoint site and list to pull the data into Power BI.

faq.question: Can I refresh the data automatically after the initial connection?
faq.answer: Yes, you can set up scheduled refreshes so your report reflects the latest changes made to the SharePoint list.

faq.question: Will formatting or formulas in SharePoint carry over into Power BI?
faq.answer: Power BI pulls in the underlying data values; visual formatting from SharePoint doesn't transfer, since you'll style visuals separately in Power BI.

faq.question: Can multiple SharePoint lists be combined into one Power BI report?
faq.answer: Yes, you can connect to several lists and merge or relate them within your Power BI data model.
